metadata.match_reason: AI-first digital engineering firm;  explicit RAG/LLM/Agentic frameworks (LangGraph, LangChain, AWS Bedrock Agentcore); Elite NVIDIA/AWS/GCP partner; SageMaker/Lambda stack.
metadata.notes: Multiple Bengaluru GenAI MLE postings; Levels.fyi MLE total comp ₹8.8L-22.7L+ mid-level; senior 25-35 LPA.

research_summary: Quantiphi (1K-5K employees, Bengaluru HQ for India) is an AI-first digital engineering firm. Phi Labs is their R&D hub — the division behind the open JR9410 Associate Architect - ML Engineer role. The role sits at the intersection of GenAI research and production architecture: RAG, multi-agent systems, LLM reasoning, evaluation/benchmarking. Recent signals: AWS GenAI Innovation Center First Preferred Partner (April 2026), Candyspace acquisition (March 2026), Phi Labs launch (May 2025). Email pattern: firstname.lastname@quantiphi.com. 8 contacts mapped across tiers 1/3/4.
jd_full_text:
Associate Architect - Machine Learning Engineer
JR9410 | IN KA Bengaluru | Full time | Posted 16 Days Ago | End Date: May 30, 2026

While technology is the heart of our business, a global and diverse culture is the heart of our success. We love our people and we take pride in catering them to a culture built on transparency, diversity, integrity, learning and growth.

If working in an environment that encourages you to innovate and excel, not just in professional but personal life, interests you- you would enjoy your career with Quantiphi!

About Phi Labs

Phi Labs is the innovation and research division of Quantiphi, focused on advancing next-generation AI technologies and translating cutting-edge research into scalable enterprise solutions. The team works on emerging areas including Generative AI, autonomous AI agents, multimodal intelligence, and AI platform innovation.

Role Overview:

We are looking for a Machine Learning Solution Architect who will operate at the intersection of AI research, advanced prototyping, and scalable architecture design.

This role will focus on exploring, experimenting, and productionizing cutting-edge AI techniques, particularly in Generative AI and Agentic AI systems. The architect will work closely with research scientists, applied ML engineers, and platform teams to design novel AI capabilities, validate them through rapid experimentation, and evolve them into reusable AI platforms and frameworks.

Key Responsibilities:

AI Research & Innovation

Explore and evaluate state-of-the-art research in Generative AI, LLM architectures, and autonomous AI agents.

Prototype novel AI capabilities and system designs based on emerging research.

Contribute to internal research initiatives, whitepapers, and innovation accelerators.

Translate academic or open research ideas into enterprise-ready AI architectures.

Ability to interpret and implement research papers across multiple domains, including Life Sciences and Digital Twins.

Strong understanding of DevOps practices such as containerization, scalable deployments, and production-ready infrastructure.

Up-to-date with recent advancements and trends in Generative AI.

Generative AI & Agentic AI Systems:

Design architectures for agentic AI systems capable of planning, reasoning, and tool usage.

Build experimental frameworks for:
Multi-agent collaboration
Autonomous decision-making workflows
LLM reasoning and planning systems
Retrieval-augmented AI systems

Explore innovations in AI memory systems, tool orchestration, and knowledge grounding.

Rapid Prototyping & Experimental Platforms

Lead development of research prototypes and experimental AI platforms.

Develop reference architectures for GenAI applications such as:
AI copilots
AI research assistants
Autonomous workflow agents

Run benchmarks and evaluations for new models, architectures, and agent frameworks.

AI Platform & Architecture Design

Define modular AI architectures that support experimentation and rapid iteration.

Architect internal platforms that enable fast development of GenAI and agent-based systems.

Apply platform engineering principles to build reusable AI components including:
agent orchestration layers
prompt management systems
evaluation frameworks
experimentation infrastructure.

Collaboration with Research & Engineering Teams

Work closely with ML engineers to convert research concepts into working prototypes.

Provide technical leadership for AI architecture and system design decisions.

Guide engineering teams in building scalable implementations of experimental AI systems.

Required Qualifications:

5-7+ years of experience in Machine Learning, AI engineering, or AI system architecture
Strong experience with deep learning and large language models
Experience building LLM-based applications or frameworks
Solid programming expertise in Python
Experience designing distributed AI systems
Strong understanding of ML system architecture and productionization

Preferred Experience:

Experience with agent frameworks and autonomous AI systems
Experience building RAG pipelines and knowledge-driven AI systems
Familiarity with research papers and experimental AI frameworks
Experience with AI evaluation, benchmarking, and model experimentation
Experience with cloud-native architectures and platform engineering

Technologies & Areas of Exploration:

Candidates should have familiarity or interest in areas such as:
LLM ecosystems
Autonomous AI agents
AI orchestration frameworks
Vector databases and knowledge retrieval
Multimodal AI systems
AI reasoning and planning architectures
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with research-to-production GenAI and agentic AI -- Phi Labs role is about prototyping cutting-edge AI into enterprise platforms. Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques first (GenAI, Agentic AI, LLM Orchestration per JD), Languages 2nd, Vector DBs 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet (1,000+ model config evaluation shows research-to-production rigor). Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot.

contacts.contact_role: Enterprise AI/ML Architect | GenAI Platform Builder at Quantiphi
contacts.research_hook: Feb 2026 LinkedIn post arguing 'guardrails are the real architecture, not the model' — discussed production GenAI requiring orchestration layers, RAG pipelines, vector DBs, moderation filters, model routing, cost governance. Also argued for modularity: if the architecture can't swap models without a redesign, you built a solution not a platform. Both points map directly to Amit's LangGraph state machine (explicit nodes for permission filtering, retrieval, tool-calling, answer generation) and the FAISS-to-Milvus migration work.
contacts.subject: your architecture-not-model post, a quick question
contacts.content:
Saw your February post where you argued that guardrails are the real architecture, not the model. The point about modularity stuck: if swapping models requires redesigning the system, you built a solution instead of a platform.

That's almost word-for-word how I framed the LangGraph system I built at Vivansh. Explicit graph nodes for permission filtering, retrieval, tool-calling, and answer synthesis. Swapping GPT-4o for Qwen3-Coder-480B in the form pipeline was a one-config change. Same principle drove the RAG rebuild: migrated from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR went from 45% to 95% on 50,000+ queries per month.

Applying for the JR9410 Associate Architect role in Phi Labs and wanted to reach out directly. Would a 15-minute call work next Tuesday or Thursday?

Amit Gohel
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your architecture-not-model post, a quick question
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Bumping this in case it got buried. Since I sent the last email, I put together a short write-up comparing FAISS, Milvus, and Qdrant benchmark results on production-scale data. The p99 latency gap was larger than I expected. Happy to share if useful.

The JR9410 role closes May 30, so wanted to follow up before then.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your architecture-not-model post, a quick question
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Last note on this. If now's not the right time at Quantiphi, I understand. Would still appreciate being on your radar for when the timing does work out.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.research_hook: ML Architect on Quantiphi's NVIDIA Alliance team — Quantiphi is a 3x NVIDIA Service Delivery Partner of the Year and runs baioniq on NVIDIA AI Enterprise. His work sits at the Phi Labs/NVIDIA intersection, building GenAI solutions that are the exact systems the JR9410 role feeds into.
contacts.subject: NVIDIA alliance, vector DB benchmarking
contacts.content:
Came across your work on Quantiphi's NVIDIA Alliance team while researching the Phi Labs role. Building production GenAI on baioniq and NVIDIA AI Enterprise is the kind of infrastructure work I've been focused on.

For context: at Silver Touch Technologies I led a vector DB evaluation across FAISS, Milvus, and Qdrant on production data, tested 6 embedding models including BGE-M3, and rebuilt retrieval from scratch. MRR went from 45% to 95% across 50,000+ queries per month. The Milvus plus reranker combination worked, but the hybrid search configuration tradeoffs were non-obvious to get right.

Applying for the JR9410 Associate Architect role at Phi Labs. Would it make sense to chat 15 minutes about how your team approaches production RAG at that scale?

Amit Gohel
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: NVIDIA alliance, vector DB benchmarking
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Following up from last week. Quantiphi's AWS GenAI Innovation Center partnership from April, specifically the agentic workflow architecture for enterprise clients, maps closely to the LangGraph permission-aware agent I built at Vivansh. Would still appreciate 15 minutes if timing works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: NVIDIA alliance, vector DB benchmarking
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Last note. If Quantiphi isn't hiring at my level right now, that's fine. Happy to stay in touch for when it does come up.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

contacts.contact_role: Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Quantiphi | GenAI | GCP
contacts.research_hook: Senior ML at Quantiphi delivering production GenAI systems across healthcare, manufacturing, and education on GCP. MLOps focus — has shipped the same kind of multi-industry production ML work Amit has done, making this a peer-level technical conversation rather than a cold pitch.
contacts.subject: RAG at 50K queries, a quick question
contacts.content:
Going through Quantiphi's production GenAI case studies this week and your name came up a few times in the context of GCP-based ML delivery across healthcare and manufacturing. That kind of multi-industry production scope is not common at two or three years of experience.

For context: I rebuilt the RAG chatbot infrastructure at Vivansh last year. 50,000+ queries per month, 10+ chatbot instances, migrated from FAISS to Milvus and from in-code Llama to a VLLM-served API. MRR went from 45% to 95%. Led the vector DB and embedding model evaluation, tested 6 models on production data, built custom retrieval with BGE-M3 plus a reranker.

Applying for the JR9410 Associate Architect role at Phi Labs. If your team is hiring, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how you think about production RAG architecture at Quantiphi's scale.

Amit Gohel
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.follow_up_1_content:

The AWS GenAI Innovation Center partnership from April, and specifically the agentic workflow focus, lines up directly with the LangGraph system I built at Vivansh. JR9410 closes May 30, so wanted to check in before then.

Amit

A bit more context: two years building production AI, most recently a LangGraph-based agentic system at Vivansh (500+ concurrent users, 1,000+ queries per day, 250+ permission rules enforced per query across a 2-stage RAG pipeline). Quick background: B.E. Computer Engineering June 2024, then two years in production AI at Vivansh and Silver Touch. Research internship at IIT Delhi before graduating.

Applying for the JR9410 Associate Architect role at Phi Labs. Would you be open to a 10-minute chat about the hiring process and what the team looks for? No pressure either way.

Amit Gohel
